halve_fix.explanation: Use the fixed-point combinator to define a recursive halving function on natural numbers.

   --> ... --> 6 *)
fact_steps.explanation: Trace the reduction of fact applied to 3 through the fix-point unfolding and arithmetic reductions.

STLCExtended_subst.solution:
Fixpoint subst (x : string) (s : tm) (t : tm) : tm :=
  match t with
  | tm_var y => if String.eqb x y then s else t
  | <{\y:T, t1}> => if String.eqb x y then t else <{\y:T, [x:=s]t1}>
  | <{t1 t2}> => <{([x:=s]t1) ([x:=s]t2)}>
  | <{true}> => <{true}>
  | <{false}> => <{false}>
  | <{if t1 then t2 else t3}> => <{if ([x:=s]t1) then ([x:=s]t2) else ([x:=s]t3)}>
  | <{0}> => <{0}>
  | <{succ t1}> => <{succ ([x:=s]t1)}>
  | <{pred t1}> => <{pred ([x:=s]t1)}>
  | <{t1 * t2}> => <{([x:=s]t1) * ([x:=s]t2)}>
  | <{if0 t1 then t2 else t3}> => <{if0 ([x:=s]t1) then ([x:=s]t2) else ([x:=s]t3)}>
  | <{let y = t1 in t2}> => if String.eqb x y then <{let y = ([x:=s]t1) in t2}>
                            else <{let y = ([x:=s]t1) in ([x:=s]t2)}>
  | <{(t1, t2)}> => <{(([x:=s]t1), ([x:=s]t2))}>
  | <{t1.fst}> => <{([x:=s]t1).fst}>
  | <{t1.snd}> => <{([x:=s]t1).snd}>
  | <{inl T2 t1}> => <{inl T2 ([x:=s]t1)}>
  | <{inr T1 t1}> => <{inr T1 ([x:=s]t1)}>
  | <{case t0 of | inl y1 => t1 | inr y2 => t2}> =>
      <{case ([x:=s]t0) of
        | inl y1 => (if String.eqb x y1 then t1 else [x:=s]t1)
        | inr y2 => (if String.eqb x y2 then t2 else [x:=s]t2)}>
  | <{nil T}> => <{nil T}>
  | <{t1 :: t2}> => <{([x:=s]t1) :: ([x:=s]t2)}>
  | <{case t0 of | nil => t1 | y1 :: y2 => t2}> =>
      <{case ([x:=s]t0) of
        | nil => ([x:=s]t1)
        | y1 :: y2 => (if String.eqb x y1 then t2
                      else if String.eqb x y2 then t2
                      else [x:=s]t2)}>
  | <{fix t1}> => <{fix ([x:=s]t1)}>
  end
where "'[' x ':=' s ']' t" := (subst x s t) (in custom stlc).
STLCExtended_subst.explanation: Extend substitution for all new forms: let, pairs, sums, lists, and fix. Handle binding correctly (skip substitution under bound variables).

STLCExtended_step.solution:
(* Add step rules for new forms:
   - Let: let x = v in t2 --> [x:=v]t2 (when v is a value)
   - Pairs: (v1,v2).fst --> v1, (v1,v2).snd --> v2
   - Sums: case (inl T v) of inl x1 => t1 | inr x2 => t2 --> [x1:=v]t1
   - Lists: case nil of nil => t1 | h::t => t2 --> t1
            case (v1::v2) of nil => t1 | h::t => t2 --> [h:=v1][t:=v2]t2
   - Fix: fix (\x:T.t) --> [x:=fix(\x:T.t)]t
   Plus congruence rules for reducing subexpressions. *)
STLCExtended_step.explanation: Define small-step reduction rules for let-bindings, pair projections, sum case analysis, list case analysis, and fix-point unfolding.

STLCExtended_has_type.solution:
(* Add typing rules for new forms:
   - T_Let: Gamma |-- t1 : T1, (x|->T1;Gamma) |-- t2 : T2 => Gamma |-- let x=t1 in t2 : T2
   - T_Pair: Gamma |-- t1 : T1, Gamma |-- t2 : T2 => Gamma |-- (t1,t2) : T1*T2
   - T_Fst: Gamma |-- t : T1*T2 => Gamma |-- t.fst : T1
   - T_Snd: Gamma |-- t : T1*T2 => Gamma |-- t.snd : T2
   - T_Inl: Gamma |-- t : T1 => Gamma |-- inl T2 t : T1+T2
   - T_Inr: Gamma |-- t : T2 => Gamma |-- inr T1 t : T1+T2
   - T_Case_Sum: case analysis on sums
   - T_Nil: Gamma |-- nil T : List T
   - T_Cons: Gamma |-- t1 : T, Gamma |-- t2 : List T => Gamma |-- t1::t2 : List T
   - T_Case_List: case analysis on lists
   - T_Fix: Gamma |-- t : T->T => Gamma |-- fix t : T *)
STLCExtended_has_type.explanation: Add typing rules for let, pairs, projections, sums, lists, and fix. Each follows standard STLC extension patterns.

STLCExtended_examples.solution: (* Optional exercise: verify typing and reduction of example programs using the extended STLC. *)
STLCExtended_examples.explanation: Prove that example programs (factorial, map, equal, etc.) are well-typed and reduce to expected values.

STLCExtended_progress.explanation: Progress for the extended STLC: every well-typed closed term is a value or can step. Extended to handle all new term forms.

STLCExtended_substitution_preserves_typi.solution:
Theorem substitution_preserves_typing : forall Gamma x U t v T,
  (x |-> U ; Gamma) |-- t \in T ->
  empty |-- v \in U ->
  Gamma |-- [x:=v]t \in T.
Proof.
  intros Gamma x U t v T Ht Hv.
  generalize dependent Gamma. generalize dependent T.
  induction t; intros T Gamma Ht;
    inversion Ht; subst; simpl; eauto.
  - (* var *) destruct (String.eqb_spec x s).
    + subst. rewrite update_eq in *. injection H1 as H1. subst.
      apply weakening_empty. assumption.
    + apply T_Var. rewrite update_neq in H1; assumption.
  - (* abs *) destruct (String.eqb_spec x s).
    + subst. apply T_Abs. rewrite update_shadow in H4. assumption.
    + apply T_Abs. apply IHt. rewrite update_permute; auto.
Qed.
STLCExtended_substitution_preserves_typi.explanation: Substitution preserves typing in the extended STLC. Same structure as basic STLC but with additional cases for new term forms.

STLCExtended_preservation.solution:
Theorem preservation : forall t t' T,
  empty |-- t \in T ->
  t --> t' ->
  empty |-- t' \in T.
Proof with eauto.
  intros t t' T HT HE.
  generalize dependent t'.
  induction HT; intros t' HE; try solve_by_invert;
    inversion HE; subst...
  - (* beta reduction *)
    inversion HT1; subst.
    eapply substitution_preserves_typing; eassumption.
  - (* let *)
    eapply substitution_preserves_typing; eassumption.
  - (* fst *) inversion HT; subst...
  - (* snd *) inversion HT; subst...
  - (* case inl *) inversion HT1; subst.
    eapply substitution_preserves_typing; eassumption.
  - (* case inr *) inversion HT1; subst.
    eapply substitution_preserves_typing; eassumption.
  - (* case nil *) assumption.
  - (* case cons *)
    inversion HT1; subst.
    eapply substitution_preserves_typing.
    eapply substitution_preserves_typing; eassumption. eassumption.
  - (* fix *)
    inversion HT; subst.
    eapply substitution_preserves_typing; eassumption.
Qed.
STLCExtended_preservation.explanation: Preservation for extended STLC: each reduction rule preserves types. Beta, let, case, and fix all use the substitution lemma.
